title: producer_champion apply-audit artifact (v2 — weekly winner-take-all)
description: FROZEN contract for config/apply_audit/producer_champion/{date}.json (+ latest.json mirror) — produced by crucible-backtester optimizer/champion_promotion.py at the end of EVERY weekly Evaluator run, mirroring the config#1841 config/apply_audit/ per-optimizer pattern. Written REGARDLESS of outcome (promoted / no_contest / unchanged_winner_already_champion / error) — the config#2054 lesson is binding: a pointer-writer's liveness must be provable from day one, so this weekly write IS the liveness/freshness proxy for the champion-promotion engine (a correctly-held no-contest week does not touch config/producer_champion.json, so pointer mtime alone cannot prove the engine is alive). v2 (2026-07-14, alpha-engine-config-I2518): the champion-promotion policy moved from a HAC-significance/2-week-hysteresis/2-week-cooldown gated engine to weekly winner-take-all (Brian's ruling) — the v1 fields `challenger_matured_cohorts`, `sn_lift_vs_champion`, `consecutive_wins`, `cooldown_until` are RETIRED (no longer meaningful under winner-take-all, which carries no state forward between weeks) and replaced by `champion_score`/`challenger_score`. No live consumer of this artifact's fields exists outside this repo (verified 2026-07-14 by repo-wide grep across the dashboard/evaluator/executor repos), so this is a clean version bump requiring no cross-repo coordination. v1 records already written to S3 (schema_version=1) remain valid HISTORICAL documents under the frozen v1 shape (recoverable via this file's git history) and are never revalidated against v2 — new records always write schema_version=2. Additive-only evolution going forward: consumers MUST tolerate unknown extra fields; further renames/removals require another schema_version bump. ADDITIVE (same day, alpha-engine-config-I2544): `leaderboard_date_used` records which research/producer_leaderboard/{date}.json artifact was actually consulted this run, since the gate now reads the LATEST AVAILABLE leaderboard <= date (not necessarily an exact same-day match — see optimizer/champion_promotion.py module docstring) and the `leaderboard_stale_gt_8d` blocked_by slug can now fire. Optional (not in `required`) precisely so v2 records already written earlier today, before this addition, remain valid without a version bump — per this same additive-only policy.

date.description: Evaluator run date (trading-day normalized) — matches the dated S3 key.

outcome.description: promoted = the pointer was moved this run (champion_before != champion_after) — the challenger's realized weekly score beat the champion's. no_contest = either arm's weekly score was unavailable this week (validity guard, e.g. thinktank_coverage not yet in the leaderboard, no resolved outcomes, or the leaderboard artifact missing/stale) — a definitional hold, never a default win for either side. unchanged_winner_already_champion = both scores were available and the incumbent champion still had the higher score, or scores tied. error = the engine could not complete evaluation this run (e.g. an unexpected exception during scoring) — the failure also surfaces via the pipeline's completeness tracker; this record is the durable trace. `demoted`/`held_insufficient_data`/`held_cooldown`/`held_not_significant` are RETIRED (pre-2026-07-14 HAC/hysteresis/cooldown engine) — read-tolerated for historical v1 records, never written by the current engine.

champion_before.description: The pointer's champion value read at the start of this evaluation (or the base-case default 'scanner_predictor_direct' when no pointer has ever been written). 'agentic' is READ-TOLERATED for historical continuity (the 2026-07-13 bootstrap's own audit record carries champion_before='agentic') but is WRITE-FORBIDDEN going forward — see contracts/producer_champion.schema.json. Null only on outcome='error' when evaluation aborted before the pointer could even be read.

champion_after.description: The pointer's champion value at the end of this evaluation. Equal to champion_before on every no_contest/unchanged_winner_already_champion/error outcome, and on a frozen promotion (blocked_by=['frozen']) — this artifact records a held week without ever mutating config/producer_champion.json. Null only alongside a null champion_before (outcome='error', pointer never read).

champion_score.description: champion_before arm's realized weekly top-N alpha lift vs the shared agentic-pipeline baseline (see champion_promotion.py module docstring — indirect/common-comparator comparison). Null when unavailable this week (no_contest, or outcome='error').

challenger_score.description: The challenger arm's (VALID_CHAMPIONS complement of champion_before) realized weekly top-N alpha lift vs the same shared baseline. Null when unavailable this week (no_contest, or outcome='error').

blocked_by.description: Stable slugs naming why the pointer did not move this run (non-null only on no_contest/frozen-promoted/error outcomes; null on promoted-non-frozen/unchanged_winner_already_champion). thinktank_coverage_not_in_leaderboard/thinktank_coverage_no_resolved_outcomes/scanner_predictor_direct_counterfactual_unavailable/leaderboard_unavailable/arm_score_unavailable/no_valid_*_selections are the current winner-take-all validity-guard slugs (config-I2518). leaderboard_stale_gt_8d (config-I2544, 2026-07-14) = the latest research/producer_leaderboard/{date}.json available at or before `date` is more than 8 calendar days older than `date` — the gate now reads the LATEST AVAILABLE leaderboard (not an exact same-day match, since the writer is an async advisory child SF that may lag or fail), but still refuses to score against evidence this stale. frozen = --freeze suppressed the write even though the challenger won. unclassified_error = outcome=error with no more specific reason. insufficient_matured_cohorts/cooldown_active/not_significant_hac_adjusted/hysteresis_not_satisfied are RETIRED (pre-2026-07-14 HAC/hysteresis/cooldown engine) — read-tolerated for historical v1 records only. leaderboard_stale is RETIRED (pre-I2544 exact-date-only read, same day as the v2 bump) — fired when the artifact's self-reported date disagreed with an exact-match run_date key; the latest-available read no longer requires an exact match, so this condition can no longer occur (superseded by leaderboard_stale_gt_8d for the age-bound case) — read-tolerated only.

challenger.description: Which arm was evaluated as the challenger this run (the complement of champion_before under the current two-arm VALID_CHAMPIONS set). 'agentic' is read-tolerated for historical v1 records only.

freeze.description: Whether this run was invoked with --freeze. When true, the pointer write is always suppressed even if the challenger won this week (this record is still written unconditionally).

leaderboard_date_used.description: ADDITIVE (alpha-engine-config-I2544, 2026-07-14). The date of the research/producer_leaderboard/{date}.json artifact actually consulted this run — the latest available <= `date` (see optimizer/champion_promotion.py::find_latest_research_producer_leaderboard_date), or null when no artifact <= `date` was available at all, or when evaluation aborted before scoring (outcome=error). Optional/nullable so pre-I2544 v2 records (written earlier the same day, before this field existed) remain valid without a version bump. Always present (never omitted) on any record built by the current engine, on every outcome including error.
